description: §45 / §59.4 — a map whose KEY type is a struct containing a function-typed field SHALL fire E-EQ-003. `<m>: [Handler: int]` keys by `Handler:struct = { name: string, onFire: () => void }`; functions have no structural equality (§45.2), so the key is not §45-comparable and the map is rejected. Reject-path (pos) half of the E-EQ-003 pin (map-key variant, `type-system.ts` `classifyMapKey`); clean neg is `equality/map-key-comparable-clean` (a struct key with only comparable fields → silent). (The struct decl also fires the sibling E-STRUCT-FUNCTION-FIELD; the codes assertion is a superset, so the sibling is not asserted.)
rationale: §59.4 / §45.2: a map key must be §45-comparable so that two structurally-equal keys are the same key; a key type containing a function-typed field is not comparable and is E-EQ-003 (the function-specific case, distinct from the general E-MAP-KEY-NOT-COMPARABLE).
